subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF, World Bank, V-Dem, RSF, Freedom House, HRW, Amnesty, ACLED, OCHA) + international press; Mozambican state media EXCLUDED there, with economics IMF-validated.

A disputed October 2024 election triggered the worst unrest since the civil war (~300+ killed), while an ISIS-linked insurgency in gas-rich Cabo Delgado has repeatedly stalled a transformative ~US$20bn+ LNG project.

Standout drags: post-election violence under entrenched FRELIMO rule, the Cabo Delgado insurgency, a 'hidden-debt' default legacy, and extreme cyclone exposure; the offset is the large, delayed LNG upside.

economic.notes: A 'hidden-debt' default legacy and weak fiscal base, against a large but insurgency-delayed LNG upside.

fiscal_state.notes: A weak revenue base, high debt, and the 2016 'hidden-debt' default legacy, on IMF support and pinning hopes on LNG revenue. (IMF reviews; World Bank)

monetary_independence.notes: A managed metical with limited central-bank independence and FX fragility. (Bank of Mozambique; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The metical is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Brought into single digits under tight policy - a relative bright spot. (IMF; Bank of Mozambique)

banking_stability.notes: Thin and fragile, scarred by the hidden-debt crisis. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Coal, aluminium, and a transformative ~US$20bn+ LNG endowment - but repeatedly disrupted by the Cabo Delgado insurgency (TotalEnergies force majeure lifted/relifted). (Reuters; IMF)

institutional.notes: Entrenched FRELIMO capture with weak rule of law and a pressured press.

rule_of_law.notes: FRELIMO capture, no real judicial independence, and pervasive corruption exposed by the hidden-debt scandal. (Freedom House; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: Weak, with the hidden-debt episode a transparency black mark; usable where IMF-engaged. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Weak and patronage-laden, with limited reach in the conflict-hit north. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: A poor RSF standing, with journalists attacked covering the unrest and Cabo Delgado. (RSF; CPJ)

political_social.notes: Post-election violence under entrenched rule, with northern displacement and high poverty.

political_stability.notes: A disputed Oct 2024 election kept FRELIMO (in power since 1975) in office and triggered the worst unrest since the civil war (~300+ killed); a 2025 dialogue eased but did not resolve it. (Al Jazeera; ACLED)

civil_liberties.notes: A violent crackdown on post-election protests - killings, mass arrests, and an internet shutdown. (Amnesty; HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Strained by internal displacement; not a destination. (OCHA; UNHCR)

social_cohesion.notes: North-south and FRELIMO-RENAMO cleavages, the Cabo Delgado insurgency, and ~70% poverty. (World Bank; ACLED)

demographics.notes: Young and fast-growing but very poor, with a weak human-capital base. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A non-aligned actor reliant on foreign troops against an ISIS-linked insurgency.

alliance_reliability.notes: Non-aligned with broad partners (Western LNG investors, Rwandan/SADC troops in the north, China), reliant on outside security help. (Reuters; ICG)

conflict_involvement.notes: An ISIS-linked (ISWAP/Ansar al-Sunna) insurgency in Cabo Delgado since 2017 - resurgent attacks, ~1M displaced, dependent on Rwandan/SADC forces. (ACLED; HRW)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Not broadly sanctioned, but US sanctions hit hidden-debt figures and FX is constrained. (US Treasury; IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Among the world's most cyclone-exposed, with weak healthcare and infrastructure.

climate.notes: Top-tier Germanwatch CRI (Idai/Kenneth/Freddy) - cyclones plus coastal sea-level plus flood-drought, very low readiness. (Germanwatch CRI; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Weak and low-spend, repeatedly storm-damaged with cholera outbreaks. (WHO; OCHA)

infrastructure.notes: Poor and cyclone-battered, though the Nacala/Maputo corridors and gas hold upside. (World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: High - insurgent violence in the north, kidnapping in Maputo, and Channel piracy. (ACLED; OSAC)

disaster_insurance.notes: Negligible penetration against extreme cyclone/flood exposure. (inference)

summary: A southern African state where a disputed October 2024 election kept FRELIMO (in power since 1975) in office and triggered the worst unrest since the civil war (~300+ killed before a 2025 dialogue eased it), while an ISIS-linked insurgency in gas-rich Cabo Delgado has repeatedly stalled a transformative ~US$20bn+ LNG project and displaced ~1M people. The 2016 'hidden-debt' default still scars its credibility and institutions. Among the world's most cyclone-exposed countries (Idai, Freddy, Chido), with weak healthcare and high poverty. The large, delayed LNG upside is the lone major offset - leaving a negative profile with a long-fused resource-driven recovery option.
